t_ch: 这「工造司」的洞天早在星核侵蚀后就停摆啦。这些人怎么不搭乘星槎去安全区呢？
t_en: The Artisanship Commission delve should have suspended operations after the Stellaron corrosion began. Why haven't these people taken starskiffs to safety?

t_ch: 可能工造司比较爱岗敬业吧。想想太卜司的卜者，什么叫没有对比就没有差距啊……
t_en: Maybe the Artisanship Commission are just more dedicated to their work? The Divination Commission's diviners aren't exactly a hard act to follow...

t_ch: 毕竟是工作嘛，要靠它生活的。成年人的世界…呵呵，没有轻松二字。
t_en: *sigh* At the end of the day, work is work — you need it to live. Y'know March, adults forget what "relaxation" means after a certain age, hehe...

t_ch: 杨叔你这过来人的语气……
t_en: Sounds like you're speaking from experience...

t_ch: 有感而发，有感而发。
t_en: Just speaking from the heart, that's all.

t_ch: 开拓之路也没有轻松二字。
t_en: The Path of Trailblaze doesn't seem to allow for any "relaxation" either.

t_ch: 杨叔那是有感而发，你说这个就叫矫情啦。
t_en: I think they call that "complaining from the heart."

t_ch: 当今这世道，又有哪条命途是轻松的呢～
t_en: Are any of the Paths in this world easy to tread?

t_ch: 没准他们是夹道欢迎咱们。
t_en: Maybe they're here to welcome us?

t_ch: 是啦是啦～恩公是将军请来的援手，放在故事里便是从天而降、救急救难的御史大人了。
t_en: That's it~! First you arrive out of the blue, then the general recruits you to the cause — it's like the stories where imperial heroes bring salvation to the world.

t_ch: 只可惜这些人见了咱们，却没献花问好——恩公的猜测未免过于自信啦～
t_en: Still, it doesn't look like they're rolling out the red carpet... We might be getting ahead of ourselves.

t_ch: 各位瞧，这「工造司」里尽是研造奇械机关的工坊，与挖空心思发明创新的匠人。
t_en: The Artisanship Commission is full of workshops, buildings, state-of-the-art mechanisms, and craftsmen designing brand-new concepts.

t_ch: 他们的传统便是隔三差五捅几个篓子——要么将洞天楼阁凭空变走，要么是机巧人偶暴动什么的。
t_en: Periodically stirring up trouble is part of their tradition... entire buildings vanishing into thin air, puppet riots — you get the picture.

t_ch: 恩公们可得小心了。依我看，里头怕是出了什么天大的麻烦，叫这伙匠人去不敢去，逃又无路可逃。
t_en: It seems like the craftsmen are too afraid to go in, but they have nowhere to run to either. We should take care, benefactors — something terrible must have happened.

t_ch: 但我们总是得往里走的，先找个人打听打听吧。
t_en: Either way, we'll have to go in. Let's ask around first.

t_ch: 公输师傅……
t_en: Master Gongshu...

t_ch: ——是云骑军派来的救援吗？！
t_en: *gasp* Are you guys Cloud Knights? Are you here to save us?

t_ch: 噢，瞧这身打扮…白高兴一场，原来是旅客啊。
t_en: ...Your clothes. *sigh* I knew I was getting ahead of myself — you're just tourists.

t_ch: 在罗浮碰上这种意外，真替你们伤心。唉，我也伤心……
t_en: Sorry this crisis came during your trip. *sigh* I'm sorry it came at all...

t_ch: 不必伤心，救星这就来了。
t_en: Worry no more. The knight in shining armor is here to the rescue.

t_ch: 老乡，我是将军请来的救兵。
t_en: Fret not. I'm the cavalry the general sent for.

t_ch: 要知道伤心总是难免的…
t_en: There comes a time when we all feel sorry...

t_ch: 对，有什么困难就跟咱说～嘻嘻，你们的景元将军可是三请四邀，才说动我们来帮忙的哦，你就放一百个心吧。
t_en: Yeah, you just need to tell us what the trouble is, hehe~ General Jing Yuan pulled out all the stops to get us here — you can trust us.

t_ch: …将军三请四邀？你们？别逗了，有罗浮的云骑军在，将军怎么可能派外人来啊。
t_en: ...You mean the general invited you? Is that a joke? The Luofu has the Cloud Knights — why would he send outsiders?

t_ch: 工造司这儿出了大事，严肃着呢，可不兴开玩笑啊姑娘。
t_en: There's been a serious incident in the Artisanship Commission — nobody's in the mood for jokes, miss.

t_ch: 别在意，朋友，我们是真心想帮助大家，能跟我们说说这儿的情况吗？
t_en: You misunderstand, young sir — we genuinely want to help. Can you tell us what happened here?

t_ch: 你们在这里做什么？
t_en: What are you doing out here?

t_ch: 还能做什么，等着呗。里面危险，我们就逃出来了。
t_en: Waiting around, that's what. We ran away, it was way too dangerous in there.

t_ch: 我劝你们啊，要是还想活命，就别进去。
t_en: Please, if you plan on living a little longer, don't go in there.

t_ch: 里面怎么了？
t_en: What happened in there?

t_ch: 工造司里闹了树灾。不知谁种的盆栽突然猛长起来，枝条有两人合围这么粗！在司里到处窜高走低，几乎要把这地方捣烂了。
t_en: There's been some kind of botanical disaster — it was like an ornamental tree somewhere suddenly started to grow. There were branches so thick you couldn't put your arms around them! It spread everywhere — the whole Commission is being destroyed.

t_ch: 我也纳闷，工造司只研究机巧匠艺，和无土栽培、快壮高之类的种植技术不搭边啊。这玩意是哪来的？
t_en: I don't get it, the Artisanship Commission focuses on the mechanical — we've got nothing to do with hydroponics or accelerated growth... Where did that thing come from?

t_ch: 快点离开这儿吧。
t_en: You should leave.

t_ch: 不行啊，我师傅还在里面呢！
t_en: The worst thing is... my master is still inside!

t_ch: 公输先生是工造司「镕金坊」里资格最老的匠人，也是带咱们这个课题组的师傅。
t_en: Master Gongshu is the most experienced craftsman in the Artisanship Commission's smelting works — he's in charge of our research project.

t_ch: 出事的时候，他拉着我、子铭、云澈几个往外头逃。结果到了半路，他自己又转身往里面冲去了。
t_en: When the incident happened, he dragged me, Ziming, and Yunche to safety... But then he turned around and ran back in.

t_ch: 我得在这儿等云骑军过来，赶紧通知他们救人。
t_en: I need to wait here for the Cloud Knights so I can tell them to rescue him.

t_ch: …他干嘛要跑回去啊？
t_en: ...Why did he run back in?

t_ch: 为了抢救成果吧？
t_en: To save his research data?

t_ch: 啥成果能比自己的命还重要啊？
t_en: Is research data more important than life?

t_ch: 是工期死线要到了吧？
t_en: Project deadline approaching?

t_ch: 咱们是正儿八经搞研究的，没有这种东西。
t_en: That's not how it works — the research comes first here.

t_ch: 你们几个就没架住他？
t_en: Did none of you try to stop him?

t_ch: 来不及啊。那紧要关头，恨不能把手当脚用，再凭空多生出几条腿来。
t_en: We didn't have time — we were scrambling for our lives! If I had an extra pair of arms, maybe I could've tried.

t_ch: 我当时只听他嘴里喊着「完啦！炉子完啦！」。等咱们几个缓过气来，人早没影了。
t_en: I just heard him shout "It's gone! We lost the furnace!" By the time we realized, he'd already vanished.

t_ch: 那个…刚刚几位说你们是来帮忙的，真的吗？
t_en: Anyway... you said you're here to help us, right?

t_ch: 千真万确，受将军委托而来。
t_en: <unbreak>100%</unbreak>. The general sent us.

t_ch: 不信也没关系～星穹列车做好事向来不留名！我们会留意你师傅的。
t_en: You don't have to believe us — the Astral Express doesn't shout about its achievements! We'll look for your master.

t_ch: 那，请拿上这张玉符。
t_en: In that case, take this jade seal with you.

t_ch: 要是你们找见了师傅，请一定平安把他带出来！他要有个三长两短——
t_en: If you find my master, please bring him out safe and sound! If anything happens to him...

t_ch: 放心，小伙子，你师傅的安全……
t_en: Don't worry, young sir. Your master's safety is our—

t_ch: ——咱们这组人今年都毕不了业，出不了师了……
t_en: None of us will be able to graduate this year...

t_ch: …走吧，不大想接着聊了。
t_en: Let's go, I think this conversation's run its course...

t_ch: 树灾？应该是「建木」吧……
t_en: "Botanical disaster"? It must be the Ambrosial Arbor...

t_ch: 但他师傅念叨的「炉子」又是……？
t_en: ...but what about the "furnace" his master mentioned?

t_ch: 这玩意不像草木之属，倒有股子金铁的坚韧感……
t_en: It's no ordinary tree — the branches felt tough as metal...

t_ch: 师姐，你有见过性质如此罕见的材料吗？
t_en: Yunche, have you ever seen material like that before?

t_ch: 见过怎样，没见过又怎样？难不成你还准备从那巨木上刨一块下来不成？
t_en: What if I have — you gonna go get a sample for me?

t_ch: 您别说，我还真存着这心思。那就是棵盆栽嘛。
t_en: I was thinking about it, yeah. Isn't it just an ornamental tree?

t_ch: 你长这么大见过盆栽走路？见过盆栽把地板凿个对穿？
t_en: Have you ever seen an ornamental tree move? Have you ever seen one bore through the ground like that?

t_ch: 就算它会动也不足为惧。咱飞快地跑上去，剌它一刀就走。实验材料就这么到手啦，它长得还能比咱跑得快？
t_en: So what if it moves? I just need to run over and saw off a sample. It can't grow faster than I can run, right?

t_ch: 来，师姐疼你，撬棍和切割锯都给你准备上了，去吧！事后云骑军把你从墙上铲下来的时候，别怪师姐没警告你。
t_en: Sure, go ahead. Here's a crowbar and a saw! Don't say I didn't warn you when the Cloud Knights have to scrape you off the walls.

t_ch: 小弟最近…偶感风寒…大师姐，材料组就属您手脚麻利，这动手实操的环节……
t_en: Well, I... I—I've been feeling a bit under the weather lately. *cough* Yunche, you're the most agile person on the materials team! When it comes to operational efficiency—

t_ch: 风寒来得挺及时哈？麻利个屁，我还想活久一些呢！
t_en: Under the weather!? How convenient. As for me — I'd rather stay alive, thanks!

t_ch: 果然，占卜结果没错。
t_en: As expected, the divination was correct.

t_ch: 要不是我今天把那「换境画屏」翻出来捣鼓，大家都得困死在里面，堆成肥料了。
t_en: If I hadn't repaired the Shifting Screen today we'd all have been trapped — we'd be fertilizer by now.

t_ch: 但这传送距离还是不太够啊……
t_en: Still, the teleportation range wasn't enough...

t_ch: 大门紧闭，一如铸成它的金铁，满是拒人千里之外的冷漠。
t_en: The gate is closed. Just like the metals from which it was forged, it emanates a cold indifference.

t_ch: 大门锁了。老规矩，咱们撬锁，还是翻进去？
t_en: *sigh* Another locked gate. Time for the old dilemma — break in or climb over?

t_ch: 先得提醒恩公们一嘴，工造司是仙舟机要重地，与太卜司同列。
t_en: A warm reminder, benefactors, that the Artisanship Commission is as important to the Xianzhou as the Divination Commission.

t_ch: 按律法，未经许可擅闯工造司，少则判上个三五百年，重则流徙几千光年以外的蛮荒世界……
t_en: Our law states that trespassing into the Artisanship Commission can result in a prison sentence of between <unbreak>300</unbreak> to <unbreak>500</unbreak> years... Serious violations may result in exile on a barren planet, thousands of light-years away...

t_ch: 非常时期还讲究这个？
t_en: I'm sure they'll be lenient given the circumstances...

t_ch: 咱们是将军的人，就没点特权？
t_en: The general sent us here — surely we get a pass?

t_ch: 有是有。只是工造司中遍布许多示警机关，一旦被机关奇械围住，将军一时间怕也是不能到场救人。
t_en: Yes, we do. Still, the Artisanship Commission is filled with security mechanisms. Even the general won't be able to save us in time if we get trapped.

t_ch: 我今日便要做那法外狂徒！
t_en: It's law-breaking time!

t_ch: …那也只是违法的时候狂一狂，该咋判还是咋判呀……
t_en: ...You gonna be this enthusiastic when it's jail-sentencing time?

t_ch: 我倒不是吓唬恩公。是建议您去工造司匠人处打听，看看能否像造访太卜司那样，找个青雀妹妹那样的司内人。
t_en: I'm not trying to scare you — I'm simply suggesting you ask the craftsmen to see if they can provide an insider like Qingque to guide you.

t_ch: 连里面是何情况都不晓得就闯进去，小女子觉得多少有些鲁莽。
t_en: It seems more than a little reckless to charge in when we don't know what's ahead of us.

t_ch: 星核促成了「建木」的重生……
t_en: The Stellaron facilitated the resurrection of the Ambrosial Arbor...

t_ch: 随着它的生长，侵蚀现象也变得更严重了。
t_en: The severity of the corrosion becomes worse as it grows.

t_ch: 仔细一看，根须都从地底下钻出来了呀。恩公，咱们少不得要当一回免费的园丁啦。
t_en: *sigh* Look, the roots are coming up through the ground. I'm afraid we've got some gardening work to do.

t_ch: 工造司乃机要重地，贼人退去，速～速～退去～呀呀呀呀！
t_en: Upon Commission grounds your feet do tread! Leave from whence you came or soon be dead! Deaaaad!!!

t_ch: 别过来！再往前走，别怪老夫不客气了！
t_en: Stay away! If you come any further, I'll not spare you!

t_ch: 喂喂，你倒是听我们解释啊！我们不是坏——
t_en: Hold on, let us explain! We're not—

t_ch: 有什么好解释的！无非是「路过」「不小心」「门开着」这种糊弄人的借口。
t_en: What excuses do you have? Surprise me! Just passing by? Stumbled in? Door wide open? HMM?

t_ch: 今天司内突然遭灾，老夫料定是有人捣鬼！果然不出所～料啊！
t_en: Within Commission grounds today was sewn, an evil spun by hands unseen, unknown! No longer!

t_ch: 安保指望不上，就让你们尝尝这机巧的厉害！
t_en: If the security mechanisms didn't teach you a lesson, these robots will!

t_ch: 「浓云金蟾」！「灯昼龙鱼」！…你们怎么了！快快站起来啊！老夫像对待亲生骨肉一样教你养你……
t_en: Golden Cloud Toad! Illumination Dragonfish! ...Are you alright? Wake up! I raised you like my own flesh and blood...

t_ch: 动手便动手，为何将这些陪伴老夫的小物捶得稀烂啊——赔给我！
t_en: Did you really have to destroy my little friends? I want compensation!

t_ch: 你自己非要不听解释的嘛！我们是来救人的，你二话不说就打……
t_en: You didn't want to listen to us! We were here to rescue you and you started a fight for no reason!

t_ch: 你是公输师傅吧？
t_en: Are you Master Gongshu?

t_ch: 小娃儿居然知道老夫名字？
t_en: How do you know my name, child?

t_ch: 老夫「镕金铄铁第一巧手」公输梁的名气这么响亮了？
t_en: Does the reputation of Gongshu Liang, "the smelter extraordinaire," precede one?

t_ch: 这位师傅的性格好单纯……
t_en: This guy is pretty deluded for a "master..."

t_ch: 你是阿伟他们的老师吧？
t_en: Are you Awei's teacher?

t_ch: 哼，是那几个不成器的弟子托你来的？没用没用，弃司内宝贵财产不顾，临阵脱逃。
t_en: Hmph! Did those hopeless brats send you here? It won't work! They abandoned the Commission's precious assets and ran off to save their own little skins!

t_ch: 就算对我有救命之恩也没用，实习统统不及格！
t_en: Even if you rescue me, it won't change anything — all of them will fail their internships!

t_ch: …不是，救人这么单纯的事儿哪那么多心眼……
t_en: ...It's not like that. You're making this too complicated...

t_ch: 其实，我们是受景元将军和符太卜所托前往「丹鼎司」，故而路过此地。想请公输师傅为我们指路。
t_en: We're passing through on our way to the Alchemy Commission at the behest of General Jing Yuan and Diviner Fu. We'd like you to show us the way, Master Gongshu.

t_ch: 噢？你是将军派来的人？一场误会，一场误会！罢了，那些小玩意儿，坏了就坏了！
t_en: R—Really? The general sent you? Then this must be a mere misunderstanding! Ah — don't worry about those things, easy to fix!

t_ch: 但老夫实在爱莫能助啊，这工造司被一只不知何来的木精邪祟占据！连司里的至宝，「造化洪炉」也被那木妖窃夺了。
t_en: But there's not much I can do to help! The Artisanship Commission was suddenly taken over by some evil tree demon. It seized our most precious possession — the Creation Furnace!

t_ch: 那妖物可不得了！还能让司里机巧之物突然都像有了意志似地围着它转。谁上去怕是都得白白送命！
t_en: It's a dark and powerful entity. The robots... it was like they gained consciousness — they were moving in a circle around it. I fear approaching it is tantamount to suicide!

t_ch: 可老夫思来想去，也不能就这么走嘞。毕竟那「洪炉」里封着……
t_en: But then, I couldn't just run away... knowing what lies sealed in the furnace...

t_ch: 省点时间吧，去哪，打谁？
t_en: We're short on time. Where are we going? Who are we fighting?

t_ch: 老规矩是吧，互帮互助。
t_en: One good turn deserves another.

t_ch: 开拓任务也该有点新意了…
t_en: I think I know where this one is going...

t_ch: 哈哈，老夫心里有数，景将军既请各位来这儿，那是必有深意，十成十是来解这工造司之危局的。
t_en: Haha, I'm sure General Jing Yuan must have believed wholeheartedly that you could save the Artisanship Commission from this crisis.

t_ch: 我有个办法帮你们，但相对的，你们也得帮帮我。来，这边厢有请～
t_en: Means I know of may yet serve your fight, pray you in repayment aid my plight. Come! Come on, through here~

t_ch: 你们瞧，这是工造司一直未完成的机关：「换境画屏」。
t_en: Look, this is one of the Artisanship Commission's prototypes: The Shifting Screen.

t_ch: 得亏我让弟子把这玩意儿搬出来调试。木精入侵工造司时，学徒们才能逃得出去。
t_en: Good thing I got the students to take it out and make a few adjustments — otherwise they'd never have managed to get out in time.

t_ch: 你们来时都见过玉界门吧？这「换境画屏」与玉界门同等效用，能将两个空间坐标以某种，咳咳，外人听不懂的方式连接在一起。
t_en: You all saw the Jade Gate on your way in, I assume? The Shifting Screen works in the same way. It connects two spatial coordinates in ways that laymen, ahem, wouldn't understand.

t_ch: 那岂不是能瞬移？
t_en: Like teleportation?

t_ch: 那还坐什么星槎？
t_en: Then what do you need starskiffs for?

t_ch: 确实。除了传送距离太短，消耗能量太大之外，这宝贝完全可以取星槎而代～之！
t_en: Indeed. With limited range and energy costs aside, to every starskiff soon shall woe betide~!

t_ch: 那不就是「完全不可以」吗？还不如「开拓」的界域定锚呢……
t_en: You sure about that? Even our trailblazing Space Anchors sound better.

t_ch: 谢谢工造司的师傅们高抬贵手嗷～得亏有这些缺点，给天舶司留点颜面。
t_en: Thank goodness you take the time to perfect these inventions, master! If not, the Sky-Faring Commission would've become quite redundant by now.

t_ch: 本来，借着这玩意我能将各位送到对面去。谁知一个不注意，画屏竟被那木精的枝条给缠住了～啊！
t_en: Ordinarily I could've used this beauty to teleport you thither, but... Through earth and air the wretched branches boiled, the Shifting Screen entangled thence... encoiled!

t_ch: 公输梁摆了个身段，眉毛一挑，似在暗示你们问下去。
t_en: Gongshu Liang strikes a pose and raises an eyebrow as if to say: "keep asking."

t_ch: 那么公输师傅一定有办法吧？
t_en: I'm sure you know how to fix it.

t_ch: 嗯，你问得好啊，孺子可教！
t_en: How observant — you would make an excellent student.

t_ch: 多谢老师傅告知实情，告辞！
t_en: Thanks for the colorful explanation. Bye!

t_ch: 等、等等！你们要对老夫有信心！
t_en: H—Hold on! Have some faith in me!

t_ch: 这就回禀将军，此路不通！
t_en: Then I shall inform the general that this path is a no-go.

t_ch: 等、等等！年轻人，你可是小瞧了工造司啊。将军远在天边，帮不了你们，但老夫可以！
t_en: H—Hold on! My young friends, don't underestimate the Artisanship Commission. The general is too far off to help us now, but I can!

t_ch: 咱们工造司有和造化同功之力。哼哼，区区木精，不在话下！来，给你们瞧个大宝贝。
t_en: The Commission is more than a match for mother nature. Hmph! O piteous tree, who think thyself so strong, your match and more in strength to meet ere long...

t_ch: 木精入侵的时候，老夫第一时间就想到了这东西。
t_en: This is the first thing that came to mind when the tree demon attacked.

t_ch: 这是代号「匣里流光」的机关装置。
t_en: It's a mechanical device code-named the Luminflux Pyxis.

t_ch: 这本是受云骑军所托研制的武器，威力无比，除了……
t_en: I invented this weapon for the Cloud Knights. It's incredibly powerful, but...

t_ch: 「距离太短，消耗能量太大」？
t_en: Let me guess... Limited range? Energy costs?

t_ch: 物性守恒，有所长者必有所短嘛。嗐，但拿来治治这不开眼钻进工造司的木精，绝对是够了。
t_en: *sigh* Every light has its shadow. But this is more than enough to deal with the tree demon.

t_ch: 然后，点火。以火克木，必然无往不利！
t_en: And then, ignite. Fire beats wood — there's no way we can lose!

t_ch: 可惜，差一点点就能成了……问题到底出在哪里呢？
t_en: Agh, we were so close... What went wrong?

t_ch: 不错不错，师傅领进门，修行在个人。你可比老夫那几个没用的弟子厉害多了。
t_en: Very good! The master reveals the door, but the apprentice must walk through it. You're much better than my useless students.

t_ch: 怎、怎么回事？洞天要塌了吗！
t_en: Wh—What's happening? Is the delve collapsing?

t_ch: 呼，没事啦。看来，这件宝贝还真是用对地方了，一定是那木精吃痛，浑身不爽了吧，哈哈哈。
t_en: Phew, seems like it worked! That tree demon must be writhing in pain, hahaha.

t_ch: 行了，事不宜迟，趁现在穿过「换境画屏」到对面去！我怕时间一长，枝条又长出来了。
t_en: Alright, there's no time to lose. Hurry through the Shifting Screen to the opposite side before the branches grow back.

t_ch: 你们看，「建木」的根系……
t_en: Look, the Ambrosial Arbor's roots...

t_ch: 它缠绕的是工造司至宝：「造化洪炉」！
t_en: They've encoiled the Artisanship Commission's most precious invention: The Creation Furnace!

t_ch: 小娃儿，快来帮帮老夫！咱们齐心协力把它斫断，别让它穿透了洪炉！
t_en: Help me, dear young friends! Let's hack them away together! We can't let the roots punch through the furnace!

t_ch: 小心，那东西正在变化……
t_en: Be careful, that thing is changing its form...

t_ch: 这到底……是什么怪物啊？
t_en: ...Wh—What kind of monster is this?

t_ch: 不愧是神迹，信手造化生命。这股力量，真了不得……
t_en: ...Miraculous, it creates life at will. Such incredible power...

t_ch: 这东西在守卫「建木」根须和洪炉……
t_en: It's guarding the Ambrosial Arbor's roots and the furnace.

t_ch: 这东西在守卫「建木」根须和洪炉……
t_en: It's guarding the Ambrosial Arbor's roots and the furnace.

t_ch: 鹿的脚下有更多根须钻出来了？
t_en: Look at the deer's feet — there are more roots coming up!

t_ch: 枝叶开花了？大家小心。
t_en: They're flowering? Watch out — all of you.

t_ch: 结果了？好大的桃子啊…有点想摘一个尝尝。
t_en: Huh? Peaches? I am kinda hungry...

t_ch: 三月。
t_en: March.

t_ch: 没别的意思啊，挂在那儿打烂了也挺可惜的。
t_en: I'm just saying! It'll be a shame if they get smashed to pieces.

t_ch: 伤到哪里都能复原…这也太离谱了吧！
t_en: No matter how much damage we do, it just heals itself... not fair!

t_ch: 只能暂时撤退了。跟我走！
t_en: We'll have to retreat for now. Follow me!

t_ch: 妈呀，怪不得仙舟这么恐惧「丰饶孽物」，这恢复能力根本不讲道理！
t_en: Ugh, no wonder the Xianzhou is so scared of the Abominations of Abundance — they're unstoppable!

t_ch: 停云小姐，仙舟的长生种…不会都这样吧？
t_en: Miss Tingyun, are the long-life species on the Xianzhou... all like this?

t_ch: 这头玄鹿恐怕是「建木」诞下用来拱卫它的灵物，与根系联结紧密。仙舟众生大多没这般离谱的本事……
t_en: I'm afraid this Ebon Deer was likely birthed by the Ambrosial Arbor as its guardian — it shares a close connection with the Arbor's roots. Most living creatures on the Xianzhou don't have abilities like this.

t_ch: 唉，看来只要它还在，咱们几个就寸步难行。
t_en: *sigh* As long as it's there, we can't get any closer.

t_ch: 未必。玄鹿看似能在转眼间愈合伤口，真相未必如此，也许它是从某个地方汲取补给……
t_en: I wonder... The Ebon Deer seems to heal itself instantly, but perhaps it's drawing that power from elsewhere...

t_ch: 那就打到它灰飞烟灭！
t_en: Let's blast it to smithereens!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，杨叔说的不是这个意思啦！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, are you listening to Mr. Yang!?

t_ch: 顺着根系，找到它的补给？
t_en: So if we follow the roots we can find the source of its power?

t_ch: 对对，然后斩草除根，让它吸个寂寞。
t_en: Yeah! It can't heal if we take out the source, right?

t_ch: 那怪物只在「建木」附近活动。我们四下看看，也许能有什么发现。
t_en: The monster only seems to be active in the vicinity of the Arbor. Let's look around and see if we can find anything.

t_ch: 以这头灵鹿的再生能力，我们恐怕暂时拿它毫无办法……
t_en: I don't think we're in a position to take down this deer until we've dealt with its regenerative abilities...

t_ch: …老夫有个想法，小娃儿要不去那里看看？
t_en: ...Young friends, I have a suggestion. Why don't we head that way?

t_ch: 公输先生有什么想法吗？
t_en: Any thoughts, Master Gongshu?

t_ch: 唔，那头鹿起死回生的时候，周遭的建木根须在闪闪发光……
t_en: Hmm... When the deer revives, the surrounding roots glow brightly...

t_ch: 因为公输先生躲在一旁，所以看得很清楚吧？
t_en: Great observation Mr. Gongshu. It's amazing what you notice when you're not fighting...

t_ch: 嘿嘿，老夫毕竟是一介工匠，疆场厮杀～非我所长！
t_en: Hehe, I'm only an artisan, after all. My skills in clash of swords shall find no sake, yet that which clasheth needeth hands to make!

t_ch: 方才杨先生也说了，鹿精能断续愈合，恐怕是仗着从某处汲取的能量。那建木闪闪烁烁，正像是在给它续命一般。
t_en: As Mr. Yang said, the deer monster is likely drawing power from somewhere in order to replenish itself. I'm certain that when the Arbor glows, we are witnessing that very process.

t_ch: 或许这些根系就是要害。
t_en: Maybe the root system is the key.

t_ch: 姑且一试吧，也没损失。
t_en: Let's give it a shot — nothing to lose.

t_ch: 是嘞！还记得那时为解开「换境画屏」上的根须，我们用机关烧穿了上面的缠结么？
t_en: Precisely! You remember how we untangled the Shifting Screen with fire?

t_ch: 对哦，那些根须再也没有出现。
t_en: You're right! Those roots never grew back!

t_ch: 不仅如此，它的反应就像受伤一样…或许正可以从这一点入手。
t_en: Not only that — its reaction suggested we'd hurt it... perhaps that's our starting point.

t_ch: 各位，抄家伙！咱们一起把这劳什子的根须给它铲平喽！
t_en: Take hold of your weapons, young comrades! Let's raze these roots to the ground!

t_ch: 瞧见了没有？！老夫猜得没错！就按这个法子来！
t_en: See!? My theory was correct! Keep going!

t_ch: 各位走路时且留神着……
t_en: Take care where you walk, friends...

t_ch: 要是你们听到铃铛响起，就记得离那远点儿……
t_en: If you hear the ring of a bell, keep away...

t_ch: 快看，这儿还有一堆「建木」的根须！
t_en: Quick, there's more roots over here!

t_ch: 等老夫片刻，让我把装置架上！
t_en: Give me a moment — I'll set up the device.

t_ch: 这样……应该可以了吧。
t_en: I guess we're done...

t_ch: 依老夫的猜想，「建木」的根系应该深植地底，各处都是。只能说工造司的地界里，应该清剿得差不多了
t_en: I suspect the Ambrosial Arbor's root system must have spread far underground. The only thing we can say with confidence is that we removed the majority of those within the bounds of the Artisanship Commission.

t_ch: 事不宜迟，乘着这些根须还没来得及长回来。
t_en: Let's hurry, before they've had a chance to grow back.

t_ch: 接下来要做的便是……
t_en: So, the next step is...

t_ch: 看鹿死谁手！
t_en: Say hello to our very "deer" friend! ...Get it?

t_ch: 成语用的不错，下次别用啦。
t_en: Please never do that again...

t_ch: 听呦呦鹿鸣！
t_en: Go hoof some fun!

t_ch: 成语用的不错，下次别用啦。
t_en: I feel like I'm gonna be sick...

t_ch: 猎鹿去！
t_en: Deer hunting.

t_ch: 出发！
t_en: ...Let's go!

t_ch: 各位，瞧见那缠着「造化洪炉」的根系了吗？它们都枯萎啦！
t_en: Look — the roots coiled around the Creation Furnace! They've withered!

t_ch: 这下好极了，没什么能够阻挡各位了！
t_en: Excellent! Nothing can stop you now!

t_ch: 那头鹿不行了，就趁现在，一鼓作气！
t_en: It's getting weak! Quick, finish it off!

t_ch: 炉心黯淡，植物根系呈死灰色。雄鹿倒地，化为叶片飘散。
t_en: The core of furnace was extinguished with roots fading into pale gray. The deer fell to the ground and turned into broken leaves.

t_ch: 谢天谢地，这「造化洪炉」总算无恙。
t_en: Thank heavens! The Creation Furnace is intact.

t_ch: 景元将军说，「建木」是不可思议的仙道神通……
t_en: General Jing Yuan said the Ambrosial Arbor was an unfathomable celestial blessing...

t_ch: 那头鹿看似与寻常生命无异，但即便受了致命伤也能瞬间愈合。我算是明白仙舟人为何要追随「巡猎」，铲除「丰饶」了。
t_en: At first glance, that deer could have been an ordinary life form, but the ability to heal such grave wounds in an instant... I think I finally understand why the Xianzhou decided to follow The Hunt in eradicating the Abundance.

t_ch: 不死不灭的造物，一旦放任它开枝散叶，会让整个世界的生态系统彻底崩溃——
t_en: If immortal creations were left to spread their branches and roots throughout the universe, entire ecosystems would collapse...

t_ch: 怪不得仙舟人随星舰流浪宇宙，从不在某个星球定居……
t_en: No wonder the people of the Xianzhou wander the universe, never settling on a planet...

t_ch: 杨先生很有见地。可惜八千年前，身为「求药使」的先人们不能洞见这背后的隐忧——
t_en: You are a man of vision, Mr. Yang. It's a shame our ancestors, the Elixir Seekers, were unable to see that hidden curse <unbreak>8000</unbreak> years ago...

t_ch: 也许他们中有人想到了，拒绝了{RUBY_B#药师}寿瘟祸祖{RUBY_E#}的「馈赠」。然而作为一整个文明，真的能抗拒长生不死的诱惑吗？
t_en: Perhaps some of them did reject the {RUBY_B#Yaoshi}Plagues Author{RUBY_E#}'s "gift." But how could an entire civilization resist the temptation of immortality?

t_ch: 真是讽刺，智者埋泉下，愚者长不灭啊……
t_en: Ironic, isn't it? The wise are buried while the fools remain immortal...

t_ch: 仙舟悔愧于曾受「丰饶」蛊惑，才最终决定踏上「巡猎」征途。也许这一切还有挽回的余地。
t_en: The Xianzhou regrets being led astray by the Abundance... That's why they have decided to embark on the Path of The Hunt. Maybe it's not too late for redemption.

t_ch: 多谢各位的援手。我知道你们接下来要去「丹鼎司」。那里与「建木」毗邻，瞧眼前这番景象，那里一定更为凶险。
t_en: Thank you for your help. I realize you must continue on to the Alchemy Commission, to the Ambrosial Arbor. After witnessing what happened here, I know there will only be more danger ahead.

t_ch: 去吧，各位，一路小心。老夫在此祝你们顺利。
t_en: Go, friends, and stay vigilant. I wish you a safe journey.

t_ch: 一路承你们恩惠了。剩下的麻烦，就让我和那些不成器的弟子们慢慢解决吧…实在不行，还有云骑军嘛。
t_en: Thank you for your help. Those useless students and I will take care of the rest here... If worst comes to worst, we still have the Cloud Knights.

t_ch: 为何如此在意这具「洪炉」？
t_en: Why do you care so much about this furnace?

t_ch: 都是传说了。但今日「建木」复生，显得传说也不那么荒诞…你们就当个乐子听了吧。
t_en: It's only a legend... though what with the Ambrosial Arbor having come back to life, I suppose it's no longer absurd...

t_ch: 很久以前，仙舟联盟迷航天外时，曾遭遇过许多无可想象的生命。
t_en: They say that a long time ago, when the Xianzhou Alliance was adrift in deep space, it encountered many unimaginable life forms.

t_ch: 其中有名为「岁阳」的精怪孽物，以纯粹能量的形态存世，又极其渴望体察血肉凡胎的七情六欲，故而时时穿过仙舟的洞天穹顶，蛊惑人类，寄生在身体中。
t_en: Among them were abominations known as heliobi. They existed in the form of pure energy, but craved the sensations and desires of mortal flesh and blood. Sometimes they managed to infiltrate the delves of the Xianzhou in order to bewitch humans and become parasites in their bodies.

t_ch: 据说被岁阳寄体的凡人，在无形中便被它们夺走了自由意志，变得越发狂乱。
t_en: It's said that mortals parasitized by heliobi would lose their free will imperceptibly, before gradually going berserk.

t_ch: 仙舟人与岁阳经历好一番角逐，最终困住了它们，将之封禁在一处容器中。
t_en: After a prolonged struggle, the people of the Xianzhou finally managed to trap and seal the heliobi in a vessel.

t_ch: 那便是这「造化洪炉」。先贤希望能点燃洪炉，日复一日消磨岁阳的能量，让它们化为乌有。
t_en: That vessel is the Creation Furnace. Our predecessors hoped that by burning the heliobi in the furnace, their energy would gradually be exhausted until they were completely destroyed.

t_ch: 我不知道如今这炉中是否还有妖物存在。也许是我多心了吧。不过自从我随师傅学艺起，我便每日前来打点照看洪炉的运转。
t_en: I don't know if there are demons in the furnace — perhaps it's all just superstition. However, from the day I started learning from my master, I've looked after the operation of the furnace.

t_ch: 其中的烈焰确实从未熄灭过……
t_en: Indeed, the flame inside has never been extinguished...

t_ch: 你有「丹鼎司」洞天的消息吗？
t_en: Have you heard from the Alchemy Commission delve?

t_ch: 事情发生得太突然了，我和弟子们毫无准备。
t_en: Everything happened so quickly. My students and I were caught by surprise.

t_ch: 听将军说，丹鼎司中有叛徒。
t_en: According to the general, there are traitors in the Alchemy Commission.

t_ch: 我对此倒是不意外。毕竟「丹鼎司」的丹士们曾是罗浮仙舟上，离仙迹最近的人啊。
t_en: I'm not overly surprised. The Alchemy Commission's alchemists were once closer to the Arbor than anyone else on the Luofu.

t_ch: 听说，药王秘传封闭了丹鼎司。
t_en: I heard the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus shut down the Alchemy Commission.

t_ch: 药王秘传？这个名字倒是有些年头。
t_en: The Disciples of Sanctus Medicus? Now there's an old name.

t_ch: 听说在罗浮仙舟受赐建木之初，「丹鼎司」丹士们的势力一时无二。他们能借着建木的力量，以种种奇术幻化肉身，呼风唤雨，乃至点化闻所未闻的奇禽灵兽。
t_en: They say that when the Xianzhou Luofu was first granted the Ambrosial Arbor, the alchemists in the Alchemy Commission wielded tremendous influence. Using the power of the Arbor, they transformed mortal bodies, manipulated the weather, and even brought never-before-seen creatures into being.

t_ch: 他们称自己「登仙」。可惜，仙舟上到底没有什么仙人。建木一枯萎，丹士们也没了倚仗，沦落成了只能研究岐黄之术的医师。
t_en: They called themselves "Saints." Unfortunately, they didn't possess any saintly powers. As soon as the Ambrosial Arbor withered, the alchemists lost their status and were reduced to medical researchers and practitioners.

t_ch: 那么，告辞了。
t_en: Well then, goodbye.

t_ch: 我不能和你们同行啦。
t_en: I'm sorry I'm not able to accompany you further.

t_ch: 哦对了，老夫生平不喜欢欠人恩惠，这些小玩意儿你们拿着，就当是你们为我干了这么多活儿应得的报酬。
t_en: Agh, I've always hated owing a favor. Take these with you — rewards for your tireless efforts.

t_ch: 一整排的门楼子机器人！这些家伙我在太卜司见过……
t_en: It's a whole row of those gate robots! We saw one in the Divination Commission...

t_ch: 什么门楼子…那是我工造司造来镇守各处的金人。
t_en: Gate robots...? Those are aurumatons — the Artisanship Commission created them to stand guard in various locations.

t_ch: 师傅，这东西真的能保护工造司吗？这一路都闹成这样了，怎么也没见他发挥作用？
t_en: Master, are these creations truly able to protect the Artisanship Commission? There's already been so much destruction — why haven't we seen any in action?

t_ch: …咳咳，这个批次，还没上色完毕，怎能让它们出阵？
t_en: I... Ahem! We haven't quite finished the paintwork on this batch. How can we deploy them if they're not looking their best?

t_ch: 你们是受将军请托，要去往「丹鼎司」么？
t_en: So the general asked you to head to the Alchemy Commission?

t_ch: 可惜呀，这木精一作乱，连工造司都不得不暂时封闭了。
t_en: *sigh* The Artisanship Commission had no choice but to halt operations once the tree demon appeared.

t_ch: 公输师傅手忙脚乱地架好了仪器设备
t_en: Master Gongshu sets up the equipment hastily

t_ch: （看来丹枢需要一些时间才能分析出「龙蟠虬跃」的药理。等结果的时候该做点什么好呢？）
t_en: (Looks like Dan Shu needs time to study the Draught of Draconic Surge. What should I do in the meantime?)

t_ch: （不如去不夜侯那边听西衍先生来段评书吧。这似乎是个消磨时间的好办法。）
t_en: (Might as well listen to Mr. Xiyan's analytical performance at the Sleepless Earl. This seems to be a good way to kill some time.)

t_ch: （就在这里听书吧。）
t_en: (Listen to his book analysis here.)

t_ch: （听听评书）
t_en: (Listen to his book analysis)

t_ch: （还有其他事）
t_en: (Other things to do)

t_ch: 虽说这螟蝗祸祖早已身陨，可祂孕育出来的那些蠹虫可一直都在宇宙中作乱呢……
t_en: Even though the Swarm Author has long fallen, THEIR begotten vermin has continued to wreak havoc among the stars...

t_ch: 咱们罗浮虽然没怎么受过它们的侵袭，但也算得上是有过几段孽缘。今天这一折，就是那当中的一段……
t_en: The Luofu — upon which we live — never had to endure their encroachment, but we have had some encounters and attached strings with them. The story I shall tell today is one of them...

t_ch: 你听西衍先生讲了一折评书。他讲得相当精彩，时间很快就过去了。
t_en: You listen to Mr. Xiyan for a spell. His interpretation of history is fascinating, and time passes quickly while you were entranced by the storytelling.

t_ch: 这几位云骑究竟能否战胜虫群，帮助庞费尼重建家园呢？
t_en: Could the Cloud Knights defeat the onslaught of outnumbering Swarm and help inhabitants of Pomferney rebuild their home planet?

t_ch: 嘿，您别着急…请听我下回分解。
t_en: Heh, don't get ahead of yourself... All will be revealed next time.

t_ch: （每次都说「请听下回分解」，每次都挖坑不填…这个西衍先生啊……）
t_en: (He keeps saying that, but I don't think he's ever completed a story before... Mr. Xiyan is just...)

t_ch: （看来丹枢已经研究出结果了，去找她谈谈吧。）
t_en: (Looks like Dan Shu found something conclusive — let's go and talk to her.)

t_ch: 你来了。从脚步听来，你的状态不错。
t_en: Ah, you're back. From the sound of your stride, I sense you're in good spirits.

t_ch: 确实还不错。
t_en: You've got that right.

t_ch: 许多远跨群星来到仙舟的人要么心事重重，要么疲惫不堪。你的步伐却坚实有力，不愧是无名客。
t_en: Many who undertake the far-reaching journey to the Xianzhou are weighed down by a heavy heart and exhaustion... but your steps are bold and steady - a stride worthy of a Nameless.

t_ch: 这怎么听出来的？
t_en: You can tell just by listening?

t_ch: 从足踵的擦地声，我能辨识出人的步态与步幅。就像气息反映着躯体的健康一样，步伐反映的是你的力量与心情。
t_en: A persons's footfall reveals a lot about their strength and emotions — much like how a person's breath may serve as an indication of their overall health.

t_ch: 你耳朵真厉害。
t_en: Your ears are really sharp!

t_ch: 不必羡慕，这不是天赋，只是目不视物所得到的补偿。我听到的，嗅到的，终究无法替代你眼前真实的色彩。
t_en: I wouldn't be envious. This is not an innate ability — merely compensation for my visual impairment. Ultimately, nothing I hear or smell can match the vividness of the colors that you perceive.

t_ch: 还是来谈谈正事吧…在下研究了一下你得到的那个药方，「龙蟠虬跃」。现在得到了一些初步的结论，请你帮在下送去神策府吧。
t_en: Let's get down to business... I analyzed the prescription you procured: The Draught of Draconic Surge. Now that I have a preliminary conclusion, it needs to be delivered to the Seat of Divine Foresight.

t_ch: 有什么结论吗？
t_en: What is the conclusion?

t_ch: 结论嘛…从结果上来说，在下并不认为「龙蟠虬跃」拥有「解药」。甚至在可预见的未来，也不会有任何「解药」。
t_en: The conclusion... Put simply, I don't believe that the Draught of Draconic Surge holds the cure to the mara. Even in the foreseeable future, there will likely not be any such cure.

t_ch: 在下已经将更详细的内容整理成文了，如果你感兴趣的话，可以自己看看。
t_en: I've compiled the details into a written analysis. If you're interested, feel free to take a look.

t_ch: 这是在下整理好的《「龙蟠虬跃」药理考》，若策士长急着想看，就要劳烦你呈递上去了。
t_en: This is a copy of "Pharmacological Studies on the Draught of Draconic Surge." If the chief counselor wants to see it urgently, I'll have to ask you to deliver it for me.

t_ch: 还有事么？神策府那厢不是急着想要知道药理么？
t_en: Need something else? Isn't the Seat of Divine Foresight anxious to learn about the prescription?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你来了啊。怎么，是有什么事要商量吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you're back. Anything you wanted to share?

t_ch: 关于药王秘传的药方…
t_en: The prescription from the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus...

t_ch: 这么快便有下文了？看来这几位丹士确实颇有水平。
t_en: Wow, even for those alchemists, that was fast.

t_ch: 是丹枢研究出来的。
t_en: It was Dan Shu who figured it out.

t_ch: 丹枢？等等？丹鼎司的丹士长怎么也滞留在外？
t_en: Dan Shu? Wait — why would the Alchemy Commission's chief alchemist be away from her post?

t_ch: 我听说这位大人双目失明，不良于行，平日里绝少离开丹鼎司的洞天。以她的技艺学识，难怪这么快就能解出药理。
t_en: I've heard that the chief alchemist is visually impaired — she rarely leaves the Alchemy Commission delve. She has a vast knowledge and expertise — no wonder she was able to analyze the pharmacology so quickly.

t_ch: 让我瞧瞧她的报告……
t_en: Let me see her report...

t_ch: 青镞面色凝重地看完了丹枢的研究报告。
t_en: Qingzu's expression is serious as she completes reading Dan Shu's report.

t_ch: 没有解药？啧，这些妖人到底从什么地方搞来如此高深的丹方？真教人头疼。
t_en: No cure? Tsk, where did these villains get their hands on such a complex prescription? This is infuriating...

t_ch: 不过这已是一条线索了。多谢你，{NICKNAME}，务必收下这些谢礼。
t_en: Still, this is a clue we can follow. Thank you, {NICKNAME}. Please accept these gifts for your efforts.

t_ch: 你给过我谢礼了。
t_en: You've already rewarded me.

t_ch: 那是代表神策府给你的酬劳，而这是代表我个人送给你的谢礼。你为罗浮…为仙舟做了很多事，身为策士长，这是我应有的答谢。
t_en: Those were rewards from the Seat of Divine Foresight — these are gifts from me, personally. You've done so much for the Luofu... for the Xianzhou. This is the least I could do.

t_ch: 这太客气了。
t_en: You're being too polite.

t_ch: 不必客气，你为罗浮…为仙舟做了很多事，身为策士长，这是我应有的答谢。
t_en: Please, take them. You've done so much for the Luofu... for the Xianzhou. This is the least I could do.

t_ch: 谢谢～
t_en: Thank you~

t_ch: 不必客气，你为罗浮…为仙舟做了很多事，身为策士长，这是我应有的答谢。
t_en: Please, take them. You've done so much for the Luofu... for the Xianzhou. This is the least I could do.

t_ch: （奇异的药方，外加丹鼎司洞天无由失联，其中的麻烦看来真不是一星半点。）
t_en: (A strange prescription and a sudden loss of communication with the Alchemy Commission delve... It seems there's more going on than meets the eye.)

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，谢谢你能来见我。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, thank you for coming on such short notice.

t_ch: 需要我做什么事？
t_en: What can I help you with?

t_ch: 我突然想起还有别的事…
t_en: I suddenly remember I have some other matters to attend to...

t_ch: 在下这次驻留长乐天，本是来为一些同胞看诊送药…但临时接下了神策府研析药方的活儿，耽搁了时间。
t_en: My stay in Exalting Sanctum was supposed to be focused on treating people and delivering medicine, but the Seat of Divine Foresight tasked me with analyzing the prescription... I've been severely delayed.

t_ch: 我备制了些药物，若是误了时间可不好啦，能否请你代劳，帮我送去？
t_en: I've already concocted some of the medicine in question, but there can't be any further delays. Could you assist me in ensuring it arrives on time?

t_ch: 没问题。
t_en: No problem.

t_ch: 麻烦你了。若是她遇上什么困难，请{NICKNAME}回来时也告诉我。
t_en: Thank you. The patient in question is a little girl. If she's gotten into any trouble, {NICKNAME}, be sure to let me know when you get back.

t_ch: 一定要我去吗？
t_en: Why me?

t_ch: 原本送药可以由同僚们代劳，不过眼下四处都需要医士，指望不上他们了。想来想去，也只好拜托你。就当是互惠如何呀？
t_en: My colleagues were supposed to handle the deliveries, but now healers are needed everywhere... I can't count on them to do it. I thought long and hard, but I realized I had no choice but to ask you. Perhaps you could see it as repaying a favor?

t_ch: 明白了。
t_en: Fair enough.

t_ch: 若是她遇上什么困难，请{NICKNAME}回来时也告诉我。
t_en: The patient in question is a little girl. If she's gotten into any trouble, be sure to let me know when you get back, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 配好的药在这里。她家就在悠暇庭附近，去那里很容易就能找到她。
t_en: This is the medicine. Her house is near the Court of Tranquility, it shouldn't be hard to find her.

t_ch: 小瞎子，上楼台！上得去，下不来！叽里咕噜滚下来！
t_en: Aw, little miss lady, can't see ahead, can't see right, can't see left, heads to the east, ends up west! Hahaha!

t_ch: 她下不来咯！嘿嘿嘿，下不来咯！
t_en: She's stuck, hehe! She can't get down!

t_ch: 喂，那边那个化外民，你在这儿围观什么呢？这是你该看的东西吗？
t_en: Hey, what you looking at, outworlder? Mind your own business!

t_ch: 住手！
t_en: Stop it!

t_ch: 关你啥事？你是她亲戚？不像吧？呵，真有意思，见义勇为到咱们「罗浮三少」头上来了。
t_en: What's this gotta do with you? You her family or something? Cause you don't look like it. Hmph, you're just trying to be a hero. You sure you wanna mess with the Luofu Trio?

t_ch: 「罗浮三少」是干啥的？
t_en: What is this "Luofu Trio"?

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈！这化外民真笨，都不知道咱们「罗浮三少」是干什么的！
t_en: Hahahaha! What a doofus! Who hasn't heard of the Luofu Trio?

t_ch: 「罗浮三少」就是…就是…嗨呀，就是特别牛逼的咱们仨，连地衡司的条子都拿咱们没办法呢！
t_en: The Luofu Trio is made up of two — uh... th—three super awesome kids that everyone's afraid of! Even the Realm-Keeping Commission!

t_ch: 那我去喊地衡司来。
t_en: I'll call the Realm-Keeping Commission over then.

t_ch: 有本事就喊啊！怕你不成？不就是抓进去教育一下又放出来……
t_en: Go ahead! You think we're scared of you? They'll just put us in detention — we'll be free again in no time...

t_ch: 老大，算了算了，条子不可怕，但你爹知道又要打你屁股了……
t_en: *sigh* Let's leave it, Boss. Detention's detention, but if your dad finds out, you'll get yelled at and start crying again...

t_ch: 混账！你提这个干什么！…不过你说得也有道理，咱们还是别跟这个短生种一般见识了。
t_en: Hey! Why'd you bring that up... Hmph, you're right, we shouldn't stoop to this short-lifer's level.

t_ch: 听见没有？老大放你一马，还不快滚！
t_en: Did you hear that? The boss is letting you off the hook, so scram!

t_ch: 老大是不想跟你一般见识，绝不是怕他爹打他屁股！给老子记住了！
t_en: Yeah, the boss doesn't wanna stoop to your level. Now buzz off, before he yells at you and makes you cry!

t_ch: 蠢货，你怎么还提这茬！
t_en: You idiot! Enough about yelling and crying!

t_ch: 三人嬉皮笑脸地逃开，你趁机带着小姑娘离开了这个是非之地。
t_en: While the three run away provokingly, you take the chance to lead the girl away from this site of conflict.

t_ch: 我能加入「罗浮三少」吗？
t_en: Can I join the Luofu Trio?

t_ch: 「罗浮三少」是你想加入就能加入的吗？
t_en: You think you can join the Luofu Trio just because you feel like it?

t_ch: 都说了叫「罗浮三少」，那就是只有三个人！你不识数？
t_en: It's called a trio, which means only two — uh... th—three people are allowed! D—Don't you know how to count?

t_ch: 哈哈，快看呀，这短生种居然妄想加入咱们！
t_en: Haha, look, this short-lifer wants to join us!

t_ch: 第一、不允许拥有「罗浮三少」以外的朋友，不允许恋爱，将青春献给「罗浮三少」！
t_en: You don't even know our rules! *clears throat* Section one — no making friends outside the Trio! No boyfriends or girlfriends! All members must devote their youth to the Trio!

t_ch: 第二、禁止靠做家务来赚取零花钱，一切经济来源只能靠其他小屁孩「自愿」上供！
t_en: Number two — earning pocket money through chores is forbidden — all sources of income must be "voluntary" donations from other kids!

t_ch: 第三、禁止和成年人括弧包括但不限于爸妈括弧缔结友好关系，与一切大人势力抗争到底！
t_en: Number three, no making friends with grown-ups — except your mom and dad. You must fight against all grown-up forces to the end!

t_ch: 第四、老大从不会哭鼻子。就算看到了他因为被他爹打屁股或者被黉学先生罚站而痛哭流涕，也必须要装作没看到！
t_en: Number four, the boss never cries. Sure, his dad might yell at him sometimes, and the lyceum abecedaries like to make him stand in the hallway, but those are tears of strength — not weakness!

t_ch: 混账！这第四条哪来的！
t_en: Hey! Since when did we have a number four!?

t_ch: 咳咳…不提这个。如果你诚心诚意想加入「罗浮三少」，我们倒也不是不能同意。但你得先说说，自己有什么加入我们的本钱吗？
t_en: Ahem, ignore that last one... If you really, truly wanna join the Luofu Trio, we might be able to make an exception... But tell me, what can you bring to the table?

t_ch: 我有两包零食…
t_en: I have a couple of snacks...

t_ch: 两包零食？不错啊，拿出来给我们哥儿几个看看吧。
t_en: Snacks? Not bad, let's see them.

t_ch: 浅薄的化外民，觉得用两包零食就能收买我们「罗浮三少」吗？可笑！
t_en: Fickle outworlder, you think you can bribe the Luofu Trio with a couple snacks? Think again!

t_ch: 你从口袋深处摸出两包即将过期的零食，交给了「罗浮三少」。
t_en: You produce two packs of snacks close to their best-by date and give them to the Luofu Trio.

t_ch: 肃静，先验验货。
t_en: Shh, let's see if they're tasty, first.

t_ch: 好的好的，老大，咱们验货。你一包，我一包。
t_en: I... guess it couldn't hurt. Good idea, Boss! One pack for you, one for me.

t_ch: 老大一包你一包，那我呢？我吃哪包？
t_en: Hey, what about me? Where's mine?

t_ch: 你吃屁吃！零食只有两包，那自然只有老大和我的份，你凑什么热闹？
t_en: Eat dirt! There are only two packs — just enough for me and the boss. Why would you get any?

t_ch: 老大没来长乐天的时候你就抢我零食，现在老大来了长乐天，你还抢我零食，那老大不是白来了吗？
t_en: You always steal my snacks when the boss isn't here. Now that he's arrived in Exalting Sanctum, it's payback time.

t_ch: 好了好了，你们不要吵。两包都是我的，这样就不乱了！
t_en: Quit it, you two. Both packs are mine, that way there's nothing for you to fight over.

t_ch: 老大，你怎么能独吞？咱们「罗浮三少」不是要公平分配吗？
t_en: You can't just take both packs for yourself! Isn't the Luofu Trio supposed to share everything evenly?

t_ch: 公平分配？公平分配你也没想着我啊？
t_en: Evenly? How was it even when you stole my share, hmm?

t_ch: 谁跟你们公平分配，世上就是拳头大的说了算！
t_en: Who said anything about sharing? Might makes right!

t_ch: 三个小孩瞬间扭打成一团。你趁乱带着小姑娘离开了这个是非之地。
t_en: The three children start tussling with each other. You take the chance to lead the girl away from the site of conflict.

t_ch: 我是反物质军团。
t_en: I am the Antimatter Legion.

t_ch: 反…反物质军团？别吹牛了！你反物质军团跑到长乐天来做什么？
t_en: A—Antimatter Legion? Stop bluffing! Why would the Antimatter Legion be in Exalting Sanctum?

t_ch: 来吃小孩。
t_en: We've come to devour children.

t_ch: 来…来吃小孩？哈哈…你别胡说八道了……
t_en: D—Devour children? Hahaha... N—Not funny...

t_ch: 老大…我看这家伙的表情不像是在开玩笑……
t_en: Uh, boss... I don't think they're kidding...

t_ch: 我我我我我妈经常和我说，反物质军团会吃掉不听话的小朋友…这该不会是真来了吧……
t_en: M—My mom told me the Antimatter Legion eat children who misbehave... It's not true, is it?

t_ch: 你你你你你你们冷静点！不要害怕！
t_en: C—C—Calm down! Don't let them scare you!

t_ch: 救命啊！反物质军团来吃小孩了！
t_en: HELP! The Antimatter Legion is going to eat us!

t_ch: 两个小孩拔腿就跑，留下嚣张的小孩不知所措。
t_en: The two children run away immediately, leaving the arrogant kid alone.

t_ch: 你们别丢下我啊！
t_en: Hey! Don't leave me behind!

t_ch: 三个小孩逃走了。你也带着小姑娘离开了这个是非之地。
t_en: The three troublemakers flee from the scene. Taking the chance, you lead the girl away from the site of the conflict.

t_ch: 我来招聘一名战士。
t_en: I'm here to hire a fighter.

t_ch: 反物质军团来招聘战士？哈哈哈，看来我的恶名终于传播到全银河了啊！我该什么时候来报道？
t_en: Hmph! The Antimatter Legion wants me to join them? Hah, looks like my reputation has reached the farthest corners of the galaxy! When do I report for duty?

t_ch: 老大，人家说招一名战士，又没说是来招你。人家说不定是来招我的！
t_en: Boss, they said "hire a fighter," not "hire you." It might be me they're after!

t_ch: 你们俩怎么回事？为什么不可能是来招我的？
t_en: What are you guys thinking? It's me they want.

t_ch: 就你？你有什么恶名可以让反物质军团不远万里来挖人？
t_en: Ha, you think the Antimatter Legion would come all this way for you? You're a goody two shoes.

t_ch: 你们俩吵什么！谁不知道我才是银河第一恶棍！
t_en: What are you guys talking about!? Obviously, I'm the baddest baddy in the galaxy!

t_ch: 我可没听说过会被黉学先生骂哭的第一恶棍！
t_en: Yeah, right! You think the baddest baddy in the galaxy would burst into tears whenever they're yelled at by an abecedary!

t_ch: 你再骂？
t_en: Say that again, I dare you.

t_ch: 好耶！打起来打起来！你们打起来，这个战士的名额就是我的了！
t_en: Yeah — fight, fight, fight! The winner gets to join the Legion with me!

t_ch: 要打也是先打你！
t_en: If it's a fight you want, you're getting it first!

t_ch: 三个小孩瞬间扭打成一团。你趁乱带着小姑娘离开了这个是非之地。
t_en: The three children start tussling with each other. You take the chance to lead the girl away from the site of conflict.

t_ch: 别跟我废话，离她远点。
t_en: Enough of this nonsense, get away from her.

t_ch: 你到底是谁？警告你别多管闲事啊，老子已经好几天没动过手咯。
t_en: Who do you think you are? Keep your nose out of this — I beat people up all the time.

t_ch: 让开点，别在这儿耽误哥儿几个的正事儿！
t_en: Yeah, get lost! Stop meddling in our business!

t_ch: 嚣张的小孩一边说着，一边冲上来打了你一拳。你岿然不动，他却因为反作用力摔倒了。
t_en: The arrogant kid runs up to you and punches you. You stand strong, unmoving, but the boy falls backward from the reaction force.

s_ch: 讨嫌的小孩&刻薄的小孩
t_ch: 老大！
t_en: Boss!

t_ch: 老什么大！快扶我起来！
t_en: Argh, stop yelling and help me up already!

t_ch: 我都看到了，这家伙突然用内力暗算老大，你太卑鄙了！
t_en: They used some kind of hidden power — I saw it! You're a cheater!

t_ch: 没错！要是你没用内力暗算我，我肯定一拳就给你打得满嘴血！
t_en: That's right! If you hadn't cheated, I would've knocked your lights out!

t_ch: 不是吧？我看着就是老大一拳没打动人家啊？
t_en: You sure about that, boss? They didn't even flinch.

t_ch: 你给我住口！走吧老大，咱们不跟这种不讲武德的人一般见识！
t_en: Ugh, shut up! Boss, let's get outta here — we shouldn't fight anyone that doesn't respect martial arts!

t_ch: 哼！今天先放你一马！要是公平对决，我未必不是你的对手！
t_en: Hmph! I'll let you off the hook this time — next time we fight fair and square!

t_ch: 就是就是！
t_en: Yeah!

t_ch: 三个小孩骂骂咧咧地离开了现场。你也带着小姑娘离开了这个是非之地。
t_en: The three children flee from the scene while disgruntled. You take the chance to lead the girl away from this site of conflict.

t_ch: 小屁孩这么嚣张？
t_en: So cocky, huh?

t_ch: 你到底是谁？警告你别多管闲事啊，老大已经好几天没打过化外民了。
t_en: Who do you think you are? Keep your nose out of this — I beat people up all the time.

t_ch: 让开点，别在这儿耽误哥儿几个的正事儿！
t_en: Yeah, get lost! Stop meddling in our business!

t_ch: 嚣张的小孩一边说着，一边冲上来打了你一拳。你岿然不动，他却因为反作用力摔倒了。
t_en: The arrogant kid runs up to you and punches you. You stand strong, unmoving, but the boy falls backward from the reaction force.

s_ch: 讨嫌的小孩&刻薄的小孩
t_ch: 老大！
t_en: Boss!

t_ch: 老什么大！快扶我起来！
t_en: Argh, stop yelling and help me up already!

t_ch: 我都看到了，这家伙突然用内力暗算老大，你太卑鄙了！
t_en: They used some kind of hidden power — I saw it! You're a cheater!

t_ch: 没错！要是你没用内力暗算我，我肯定一拳就给你打得满嘴血！
t_en: That's right! If you hadn't cheated, I would've knocked your lights out!

t_ch: 不是吧？我看着就是老大一拳没打动人家啊？
t_en: You sure about that, boss? They didn't even flinch.

t_ch: 你给我住口！走吧老大，咱们不跟这种不讲武德的人一般见识！
t_en: Ugh, shut up! Boss, let's get outta here — we shouldn't fight anyone that doesn't respect martial arts!

t_ch: 你给我记住！要是公平对决，我未必不是你的对手！
t_en: Hmph! I'll let you off the hook, but remember — next time we fight fair and square!

t_ch: 就是就是！
t_en: Yeah!

t_ch: 三个小孩骂骂咧咧地离开了现场。你也带着小姑娘离开了这个是非之地。
t_en: The three children flee from the scene while disgruntled. You take the chance to lead the girl away from this site of conflict.

t_ch: {F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，谢谢你帮我……
t_en: Thank you for helping me, {F#miss}{M#mister}...

t_ch: 你没事吧？
t_en: Are you okay?

t_ch: 我…没事。是我自己不好，本不该到处乱跑的…结果撞上那几个家伙。
t_en: I'm... okay... it's my fault. I shouldn't have wandered off on my own... then I wouldn't have run into those guys.

t_ch: 他们平日里便取笑我眼睛瞧不见东西，总爱在我面前使绊子，偷我的东西。
t_en: They always laugh at me for being blind. They like to trip me up, and steal my things.

t_ch: 还好有{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}在这儿，要不然他们才不会这么轻易放过我。
t_en: Th—Thank goodness you were here... Otherwise they wouldn't have let me off so easily...

t_ch: 丹枢让我给你送药。
t_en: Dan Shu wants me to deliver this medication to you.

t_ch: 啊，是丹枢大人托你来的？
t_en: Oh, Dan Shu asked you to come?

t_ch: 听说最近出了许多乱子，她一定很忙吧。谢谢{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，也请你帮我谢谢丹枢大人。
t_en: Things are bad on the Luofu recently — Dan Shu must be very busy. Thank you, {F#miss}{M#mister}, remember to thank her for me.

t_ch: 小女孩接下药，磕磕绊绊地顺着原路返回。
t_en: The little girl takes the medicine and returns the way she came.

t_ch: （她怎么孤零零的一个人来去…）
t_en: (Why does she wander about alone...?)

t_ch: （算了，药已经给到了，去和丹枢汇报一下情况吧。）
t_en: (*sigh* Well, I've delivered the medicine. I should report back to Dan Shu.)

t_ch: 怎么样？药已经送到了吗？
t_en: How was she? Did you deliver the medicine?

t_ch: 你向丹枢讲述了之前发生的事。
t_en: You describe what happened to Dan Shu.

t_ch: 她让我想起小时候的自己。在年幼的时候，我也和她一样在黑暗中摸索，要面对不知何时迎面而来的障碍，还有人们不自知的恶意。
t_en: She reminds me of myself when I was a child — I also had to find my way in the dark, face obstacles I couldn't predict, as well as deal with unconscious prejudices people had against me.

t_ch: 被欺负了，或是摔伤了，只能哭着求丹鼎司的医士为我治伤。一来二去，我自己也走上了这条道路。
t_en: If I was ever injured by bullies or after a fall, I'd run crying to the healers at the Alchemy Commission and ask them to heal me. I suppose that's why I followed in their footsteps — to pay a little kindness forward.

t_ch: 多谢你能保护那个孩子。{NICKNAME}，在下能否拜托你另一件事？
t_en: Thank you for looking after the child. Could I ask you for another favor, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 什么事？
t_en: What do you need?

t_ch: 我想去见见那个孩子，要麻烦你为我引路。
t_en: I want to meet the little girl, can you lead me to her?

t_ch: 你担心那孩子的状况？
t_en: Are you worried about her?

t_ch: 是啊。
t_en: Yes...

t_ch: 唔…我担心她一个人来去，也想听听她最近的心事。
t_en: ...I worry about her wandering around on her own, and I want to know what's been on her mind lately.

t_ch: 那个孩子的父母因为丰饶民战争而离世了…收留她的亲戚也和她不甚亲近。我为她看病时，常常成了她倾诉的对象。
t_en: Her parents passed away in the Denizens of Abundance Wars... The relatives who took her in aren't close to her, and whenever I treat her, she often confides in me.

t_ch: 医者开药也许能保护一个人的身体，但有时候心里的孤独和创伤却只有陪伴关心才能慢慢疗愈。
t_en: A healer's medicine might remedy one's physical health, but sometimes the loneliness and hurt inside one's heart needs companionship and care to heal.

t_ch: 她恐怕走远了。
t_en: She might've wandered far.

t_ch: 你知道去哪儿见她？
t_en: Do you know where you might find her?

t_ch: 她说自己有心事时，都会去工造司找个角落坐下，听听那里的声响。
t_en: She told me that whenever she's feeling troubled, she finds a quiet corner in the Artisanship Commission and just sits there, letting the sounds wash over her.

t_ch: 孩子的父母曾是工造司的工匠，想来是那里让她心有怀恋吧。
t_en: Her parents used to be artisans in the Commission, so I think she has some nostalgic connection to the place.

t_ch: 我猜她眼下是往那儿去了。不成，眼下遍地都不太平，她一个目不视物的孩子，可不能放着她乱跑。
t_en: In all likelihood, that's where she'll be. *sigh* There's danger around every corner these days — especially for a sightless child. Let's go.

t_ch: 呜呜呜…救命啊……
t_en: *sob* Help...

t_ch: 谁来救救我……
t_en: Somebody help me...

t_ch: …怎么回事？那孩子是遇到什么危险了吗？
t_en: ...What's happening? Is the girl in trouble?

t_ch: 没事了，没事了喔~小鱼。
t_en: It's alright, everything's okay now, Xiaoyu...

t_ch: 呜呜呜…谢谢{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，谢谢丹枢大人……
t_en: *sniffle* Thank you {F#big sister}{M#big brother}, thank you Dan Shu...

t_ch: 有{NICKNAME}在，不必害怕了。
t_en: You don't need to be scared anymore, {NICKNAME} is here.

t_ch: 可是我…一想到自己给丹枢姐姐…给{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}添了这么多麻烦，拖累了两位，就觉得很惭愧……
t_en: But I... When I think about all the trouble I've caused you two... I feel like such a burden... I'm sorry...

t_ch: 只是这样吗？那…你最好先学会习惯起来。
t_en: Is that why you're crying? Maybe... you should start getting used to this feeling...

t_ch: 因为在之后的几百年里，像我们这样目不视物的人，还会继续拖累我们遇到的每个人。
t_en: ...because in the next few centuries, visually impaired people like us will continue to require help from everyone we meet.

t_ch: 如果只是因为这点小小的愧疚就让你感到伤心，这条长路你还是一步也别走下去了。
t_en: So, if a little bit of guilt like this is enough to break your heart, then how will you manage such an arduous path?

t_ch: 丹枢？
t_en: Dan Shu?

t_ch: 这算是安慰吗？
t_en: Is this supposed to be comforting?

t_ch: 这不是安慰，这是…现实。
t_en: I'm not trying to console her. This is... the reality.

t_ch: 在黑暗中独行，恐惧着突如其来的障碍，不能理解别人描述的所见所闻…
t_en: Walking alone in the darkness, fearing every incoming, invisible obstacle, unable to understand other people's descriptions...

t_ch: 所有人，所有事物都像是笼罩在漆黑的炭火里，看不见却能灼伤你。
t_en: Everyone, everything is like a hot coal in the dark — invisible, yet still able to burn you.

t_ch: 这样的生活，对长生种而言还要持续将近千年的时光。她必须学会靠自己挨过去，而不是妄想任何人的救助。
t_en: As a long-life species, she can expect life to continue like this for nearly a thousand years. She must learn to depend upon herself rather than just foolishly waiting around for the next hero to swoop in and save her.

t_ch: 总会有愿意帮她的人。
t_en: There will always be someone willing to help her.

t_ch: 满怀向往倒也不是坏事。
t_en: One hopes that would be the case...

t_ch: 不过，谁能时时刻刻守在她身边？在那之前，她必须先像我一样，学会忍受黑暗。
t_en: ...but that would require someone to be by her side every waking second of every day. In the meantime, she'll have to learn to withstand the darkness — just as I have.

t_ch: 可你不是也在帮她吗？
t_en: Aren't you helping to save her?

t_ch: 是啊…我确实在努力改变她这样的人的处境。
t_en: Yes... by trying my best to change her personal circumstances.

t_ch: 不过…在那之前，她必须先像我一样，学会忍受黑暗。
t_en: In the meantime, she'll have to learn to withstand the darkness — just as I have.

t_ch: 我…丹枢姐姐…我能靠我自己，只有自己才靠得住……
t_en: I... You're right, Dan Shu... From now on, I'll do my best to rely on myself...

t_ch: 很好，希望你能永远记住这一点。永远。
t_en: Good, I hope you remember this — forever.

t_ch: 这地方太危险了，{NICKNAME}，咱们回长乐天吧。
t_en: This place is too dangerous, {NICKNAME}. Let's head back to Exalting Sanctum.

t_ch: 你和丹枢将小女孩送回了家。
t_en: You send Dan Shu and the little girl back.

t_ch: 再次感谢出手相助。
t_en: Thank you again, for all you've done.

t_ch: 像我们这样的「天缺者」，年轻时免不了遭受欺辱，即使历尽了苦难，坐上了丹士长的高位，也依然对很多事束手无策。
t_en: It would seem we Incomplete Ones are often bullied from a tender age... I endured innumerous hardships to become chief alchemist, yet there are still things that leave me feeling helpless.

t_ch: 正因如此，在下才会对她说那些话…那些话在你听来一定很刺耳吧？可是，与她未来会遭遇的一切相比，在下那番话只能算是和风细雨。
t_en: That's the reason I said what I said... You might mistake my words for being hurtful — but the truth is, no amount of words can prepare her for what lies ahead.

t_ch: 没办法治好吗？
t_en: Is there no cure?

t_ch: 仙舟没有义眼之类的技术吗？
t_en: Does the Xianzhou have the technology to help with such impairments?

t_ch: 长生种的身体状况是从出生开始就注定的。相貌妍丑，心智贤愚，身材高矮…这些全都自出生的那一刻被永久固定在了血肉基因之中。
t_en: For long-life species, these things are permanent. Whether we are beautiful or ugly, tall or short, wise or foolish — all of it is encoded into our flesh from the moment we are born.

t_ch: 许多在短生种处可以借由机巧或手术弥补的缺陷，对仙舟人反而回天乏术。
t_en: The impairments of short-life species can be corrected with ingenia or surgery, but that's an impossibility for Xianzhou natives.

t_ch: 因为无论采用何种手段，植入何等机巧，我们的身躯都会复元回最初的模样。
t_en: No matter the method employed, our bodies will eventually return to their original states.

t_ch: 我也曾不信邪，为自己装上过义眼。不过很快，被摘除的盲眼又再度生成原状，让我痛不欲生。
t_en: I too was once a non-believer — I fooled myself into acquiring artificial eye implants... Soon thereafter, my sightless eyes began to regenerate, and I was left in pain and despair...

t_ch: 而从那以后，这重获光明的片刻，反而变成了……永久灼痛我的记忆。
t_en: That brief window of sight that I regained has turned into a perpetual source of anguish — forever etched into my mind...

t_ch: 对于长生种来说，「天缺」即是永恒的苦行，避无可避。
t_en: For long-life species, this incompleteness is a permanent, unavoidable penance.

t_ch: 哼，讽刺啊…很多「丰饶民」——也就是所谓「孽物」不会受此折磨。呵，至于「天缺」，大约是寿瘟祸祖为背叛者准备的诅咒吧。
t_en: Hmph, what a joke... Even the Denizens of Abundance — the so-called "abominations" — needn't endure such torture. Perhaps we Incomplete Ones are merely traitors cursed by the Plagues Author.

t_ch: 这又是为什么？
t_en: What do you mean?

t_ch: 你的脸色变得好可怕…
t_en: You had a scary look on your face just now...

t_ch: 不，没事了…是在下一时失言。
t_en: It's nothing... Forget I said anything.

t_ch: 辛苦你陪我走这一遭，{NICKNAME}。我想，我在长乐天的心愿也暂时了却了。
t_en: Thank you for sticking with me through all of this, {NICKNAME}. I think my aspirations in Exalting Sanctum have been met, for now.

t_ch: 对了，在下有一件礼物想要送给你。但这还需要我准备一段时间…等到谢礼准备好，在下会再联系你的。
t_en: Oh, I have a gift for you... though I'll need a little time to prepare it. I'll contact you once it's ready to collect.

t_ch: 那我们就暂时别过吧，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: See you soon, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 你的脚步声逗留不去，是因为心里还有很多疑惑吗？
t_en: Your footsteps still linger. Could it be that you still have unanswered questions?

t_ch: 想问的话就问吧，我一定知无不言。
t_en: Ask, and I shall answer to the best of my ability.

t_ch: 真的没法治好「天缺者」吗？
t_en: Is there really no way to cure Incomplete Ones?

t_ch: 并非全部的「天缺」都是不治之症。在下的这只机巧臂就还算是活动自如。因为它不涉及任何侵入性的手术，只是在皮肤表面接收生物电讯号来运转。
t_en: Not all incompletions are without remedy. Take my ingenium arm, for example — it doesn't require any invasive surgery and operates by detecting bioelectric signals on my skin.

t_ch: 但在下的双眼情况不同。视神经发育不全，现在只能依靠植入物来医治，但植入物很快会被仙舟人强大的恢复能力「排异」出去…那过程生不如死。
t_en: My eyes are another story — the optic nerves never fully developed. Transplants are a temporary solution, but they are soon rejected by the superior healing powers of the Xianzhou natives... The entire process is worse than death.

t_ch: 类似的情况还有很多…骨骼畸形、软组织缺失、先天性心脏病，等等等等。
t_en: There are many similar cases... Skeletal deformities, soft tissue defects, congenital heart disease — the list goes on...

t_ch: 对于短生种来说，这些都可以靠人造器官和植入物来解决。但对于长生种来说，这些都是「天缺」。
t_en: For short-life species, all these problems can be solved with artificial organs and implants. For long-life species, however, they remain as incompletions.

t_ch: 举个最简单的例子吧…你知道吗，虽然程度不同，但仙舟人大都受到口腔疾病和颞下颌关节紊乱的困扰。
t_en: To give a common example... Did you know that to varying degrees, most Xianzhou natives are plagued by oral diseases and jaw-related conditions?

t_ch: 因为对于很多像仙舟人这样的生物来说，「智齿」都是需要尽早拔除的。可仙舟人做不到，仙舟人的智齿像竹子一样顽强，除之不绝，是一个终身的顽疾。
t_en: For most living creatures, wisdom teeth need to be removed as early possible... but for we long-life species, they are as stubborn as boulders. We must endure them for as long as we live.

t_ch: 你看，这就是「天人之舟」的真相…「天缺之舟」罢了。
t_en: You see? This is the truth about the Vessel of Celestials... nothing more than a vessel of the Incomplete.

t_ch: 为什么孽物不会「天缺」？
t_en: Why wouldn't abominations be Incomplete?

t_ch: 同受「丰饶」的转变，你可知道「丰饶孽物」和仙舟人的区别是什么？
t_en: For beings transformed by the Abundance, there's a difference. Do you know how Abominations of Abundance and Xianzhou people differ?

t_ch: 我以丹鼎司传授的学识，冥思苦想过这个问题。但内心只有一个答案。并无差别。
t_en: I've given this question much thought, using all the knowledge I've learned from the Alchemy Commission to answer it. But, deep down, I know there is only one correct answer: There is no difference.

t_ch: 唯一的差异在于，接受或背弃「丰饶」带来的长生种本性。
t_en: The only difference between us is whether we embrace or reject the long life placed upon us by the Abundance.

t_ch: 丰饶民中凶残著称的步离人，曜青的狐人体内便流着和他们近似的血脉……但他们不是「孽物」而是「仙舟人」。
t_en: Borisin, known for their ferocity among the Denizens of Abundance, are similar to the foxians of the Yaoqing genetically... But the foxians are not abominations — they're Xianzhou people.

t_ch: 由此可见，「孽物」和「仙舟人」的区别，并非是一个生物分类学问题，而是一个文化问题。
t_en: From there, we can say that the difference between an abomination and a Xianzhou person is not one of taxonomy, but one of culture.

t_ch: 孽物入仙舟，则仙舟之；仙舟入孽物，则孽物之。如是而已。
t_en: If an abomination aligns with the Xianzhou, then they are a Xianzhou person. If a Xianzhou person aligns with the abominations, they become an abomination themselves. It is that simple.

t_ch: 孽物们拥抱自己的本性，他们能毫无顾忌地改变自己的血肉，只为追求更强的躯壳。但这些手段，在仙舟之上，已是彻头彻尾的禁忌。
t_en: Abominations embrace their nature — they change and modify their flesh without care, for the sake of attaining power and strength. However, to the Xianzhou people, these methods are completely taboo.

t_ch: 现在你明白，为什么孽物中不会诞生「天缺者」了吧？
t_en: Now you know why there are no Incomplete Ones among the abominations...

t_ch: 你了解药王秘传吗？
t_en: Do you know much about the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus?

t_ch: 在下只是一介丹士，不了解太复杂的因缘。只知道这是一群自认为「丰饶民」的仙舟人。
t_en: I'm just an alchemist — I don't understand them in any detail. I only know that they are a group of Xianzhou people who view themselves as Denizens of Abundance.

t_ch: 我猜想，他们只是将太多苦难归咎于自己背离了寿瘟祸祖的命途，因而才想要靠重新成为「丰饶民」来为自己救难吧。
t_en: My guess is that they attribute their suffering to their decision to renounce the Path of the Plagues Author. Thus, they seek to save themselves by becoming Denizens of Abundance again.

t_ch: 没有更多问题了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 在下有一件礼物想要送给你。但这还需要我准备一段时间…等到谢礼准备好，在下会再联系你的。
t_en: I have a gift for you, but I'll need a little time to prepare it... I'll contact you once it's ready to collect.

t_ch: 那我们就暂时别过吧，{NICKNAME}小友。
t_en: See you soon, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 你出于好奇，信手翻了翻丹枢身旁的书册。她闻声转向你。
t_en: Out of curiosity, you flip through the books beside Dan Shu. Hearing that, she turns to you.

t_ch: 这是在下早年的日记，记录了很多自己当年战胜天缺的尝试。
t_en: This is a diary I kept — it records the many attempts I made to overcome my incompletions.

t_ch: 抱歉…
t_en: I'm sorry...

t_ch: 不知道这是你的日记啊…
t_en: Oops, I didn't realize this was your diary...

t_ch: 无妨，拿去读吧。如果你对天缺者问题感兴趣的话…收下这个吧。
t_en: No need to apologize — feel free to read it. If you're interested in the Incomplete Ones... you can take it.

t_ch: 如果它能加深你对「天缺者」境遇的了解，那也算是完成了自己的使命吧。
t_en: If it can deepen your understanding of our trials and tribulations, it will have fulfilled its purpose.

t_ch: （丹枢需要一些时间来准备给我的谢礼。现在闲来无事，不如去三余书肆看看书，等她联系我吧。）
t_en: (Dan Shu needs more time to prepare my gift. There's nothing pressing right now, why not head over to Spare Time Book Shop for a while?)

t_ch: 我想来看书打发时间…
t_en: I'd like to read some books to pass the time...

t_ch: 没问题，看吧。西衍先生说了，即使对方不买书、只看书，也绝不能赶客。宁可不赚钱，也不能寒了读书人的心。
t_en: Go right ahead. Mr. Xiyan said that making bookworms feel at home is more important than getting them to buy something.

t_ch: 不过，你最好在不碍事的地方看。不要挡住其他客人。
t_en: All we ask is that you find a quiet spot away from the browsing areas!

t_ch: 你在三余书肆看了一本联盟最近流行的小说，时间很快就过去了。
t_en: You pick up a book that has recently gotten popular across the Alliance. Time passes quickly.

t_ch: 看来你在等的人已经联系你了。慢走，路上小心。下次记得买书。
t_en: Looks like the person you were waiting for has gotten in touch. Safe travels! Oh, uh... maybe buy a book next time?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你来了。
t_en: Ah, there you are, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 这阵子你奔波劳苦，我想为你提供一些有帮助的东西。
t_en: You've been running so many errands lately — I'd like for you to take some useful items away with you.

t_ch: 研究神策府提供的证物，让在下小有灵感。我比照了相关的历史文献记载，调配了一副延寿健体药。
t_en: Examining the exhibits in the Seat of Divine Foresight inspired me to create this. I referenced some historical texts and concocted a medicine that can extend your lifespan and improve your health.

t_ch: 在下将它定名为「餐云承露丹」，想送给{NICKNAME}。
t_en: I call it the Brumedew Concentrate Pellet, and I'd like you to have it, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 服之令人身轻体捷，延寿养生。对无名客而言，一定能让你的旅途更为轻松吧。
t_en: Consuming it will make your body light, agile, and promote longevity and well-being. As a Nameless, it'll make your journey a much less arduous one.

t_ch: 好了，在下还有些事需要处理，那么…我们就此别过吧。
t_en: Well then, that's that. I've still got some other business to attend to, so... shall we say goodbye for now?

t_ch: 希望下次见面时，我能有机会看清你的模样。
t_en: Perhaps the next time we meet, I'll be able to see you in the true sense of the word.

t_ch: 丹枢将贮有丹药的小盒放在了亭台上，你看了看它。
t_en: Dan Shu placed the box with the pellet on the table. You glance at it.

t_ch: 一口吞下
t_en: Swallow it down.

t_ch: （唔…味道很奇怪，但似乎身体并没有什么不适的地方？）
t_en: (Hmm... Tastes a bit strange, but no adverse reactions so far...)

t_ch: （头突然好疼…怎么回事……）
t_en: (Wait a minute... Argh, my head... What's happening?)

t_ch: （不…不对劲，身体好热，好难受…有点喘不过气来了……）
t_en: (S—Something's wrong — I'm burning up... Feels like I can't breathe...)

t_ch: （呼…似乎稍微缓解了一些，但还是不太舒服……）
t_en: (Whew... Seems to be calming down a little, but still uncomfortable...)
